titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 599

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=936 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:51:20.464272+00 | translation moa=937 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:51:20.473907+00

p.cl: . W . dei gratia Rex Scott’ . Omnibus probis Hominibus Totius terre sue Clericis et laicis  Salutem .

Sciant presentes et futuri . me Concessisse . et hac Carta mea Confirmasse . Conuentionem factam inter Bertramum Priorem et Conuentum Dunel
mensem . et Ædwardum de Aldecambus . et filios suos de abiuratione et Quieta Clamatione quam idem Edwardus et filii
sui fecerunt predicto priori et monachis . de uilla de Adecambus . et de Molendino . et Omnibus aliis ad uillam de Alde
Cambus pertinentibus  pro maiorem lummesden’ . quam predictus Prior et monachi eis in excambium dederunt . et pro pecunia
quam predictus prior et monachi pro predicto Ædwardo erga me adquietauerunt . Quare uolo et precipio . ut predicta
Conuentio de prefata abiuratione et Quieta clamatione firmiter teneatur . sicut Cyrographum inter eos inde factum .
et Carta predicti Ædwardi testantur . Saluo seruicio meo .

Testibus . Willelmo et Waltero . Capllanis meis . Philippo .
de Valoniis . Camerario . meo . Willelmo Cumin’ . Willelmo de Boscho et Gilleberto . clericis meis
William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting.

Let present and future know me to have approved, and by this my charter to have made firm, the agreement between Prior Bertram and the convent of Durham, and Edward of Old Cambus and his sons, about the abjuration and quitclaim which Edward and his sons made to the said prior and monks of the toun of Old Cambus, and of the mill, and all other things belonging to the toun of Old Cambus, in return for greater Lumsdaine, which the said prior and monks gave to them as an exchange, and in return for money which the said prior and monks acquitted to me for the said Edward. Wherefore I will and instruct that the said agreement about the said abjuration and quiclaim be steadfastly maintained, just as the cyrograph made thereupon between them, and the charter of the said Edward, bear witness. Saving my service.
As witnesses: William and Walter, my chaplains; Philip de Valognes, my chamberlain; William Comyn, William del Bois, and Gilbert, my clerks.
At Haddington on the 8th day of July.
